2016-02-25.abstract: A Federal Court decision that prescription holders could grow their own supply for medical purposes adds to the confusion over the drug’s legal status.

2016-02-26.title: With No Unified Refugee Strategy, Europeans Fall Back on Old Alliances
2016-02-26.abstract: Without an effective Europe-wide policy response, and facing growing public anger in many countries, Europe’s national leaders have splintered, searching for allies.

2016-02-26.title: Benjamin Netanyahu Rebukes David Cameron for Criticizing Israel
2016-02-26.abstract: Israel’s prime minister responded sharply to his British counterpart’s assertion that he found Israel’s “effective encirclement” of East Jerusalem and its construction in that territory “genuinely shocking.”

2016-02-26.title: U.S. Plans to Put Advisers on Front Lines of Nigeria’s War on Boko Haram
2016-02-26.abstract: The deployment of dozens of Special Operations advisers would push American troops hundreds of miles closer to the battle that Nigerian forces are waging against the militants.

2016-02-26.title: Peering Through the Haze of China’s Economic Data
2016-02-26.abstract: Economists and academics comment on the quality and official control of economic data coming out of China.

2016-02-26.title: Decline of Pollinators Poses Threat to World Food Supply, Report Says
2016-02-26.abstract: Many pollinator species are facing extinction, including some 16 percent of vertebrates like birds and bats, according to the document.

2016-02-27.title: Syria Truce Comes With Price, but Not for Assad
2016-02-27.abstract: European and Israeli intelligence officials believe the pause in fighting may consolidate President Bashar al-Assad’s hold on power.

2016-02-27.title: Elias Demetracopoulos, 87, Dies; Journalist Linked Greek Junta to Nixon
2016-02-27.abstract: The enigmatic Mr. Demetracopoulos often exasperated the State Department, once sued the C.I.A. and was honored in his homeland as a champion of freedom.

2016-02-27.title: Suicide Bombings in Afghanistan Kill at Least 26
2016-02-27.abstract: The two attacks, in Kabul and Kunar Province, also wounded nearly 50 people days ahead of expected talks between the government and the Taliban.

2016-02-27.title: Violence in Syria Abates on Day 1 of Cease-Fire
2016-02-27.abstract: Fighting decreased significantly in the country following a truce brokered by the United States and Russia, though there were reports of scattered violations.

2016-02-27.title: Early Results Show Reformists and Moderates Drawing Votes in Iran Elections
2016-02-27.abstract: Preliminary results released Saturday showed that reformist and moderate candidates were leading in one of the two elections just held by Iran, state news media quoted the Interior Ministry as saying.

2016-02-28.title: Italy Growers Wary of Olive Oil Fraud as New Law Is Weighed
2016-02-28.abstract: Olive oil producers in the nation are troubled by draft legislation that would tinker with penalties for passing off counterfeit olive oil and its origin.

2016-02-28.title: Ex-Official Calls for Malaysian Prime Minister to Step Down
2016-02-28.abstract: A Malaysian official who has been removed from a top party job said he had seen proof that Prime Minister Najib Razak committed a crime related to the country’s sovereign wealth fund.

2016-02-28.title: Yemeni Civilians Killed by Airstrike on Market, Witnesses Say
2016-02-28.abstract: At least 30 people were reported dead after a bombing by the Arab coalition that could add to calls for an international arms embargo on Saudi Arabia.

2016-02-28.title: A Bolivian Town’s Emergence Under Evo Morales
2016-02-28.abstract: Residents of Cobija, which has prospered under ​President​ Morales, expect his leftist policies to survive his political departure.

2016-02-28.title: Bolivian Town Drifts From President Evo Morales, Despite Promises Kept to Left
2016-02-28.abstract: Residents of Cobija, which has prospered under ​Mr. Morales, expect his left-wing policies to continue even though he was denied the chance to seek another term.

2016-02-29.title: A Chilean Ex-Soldier Guiltily Recalls His Unit’s Atrocities
2016-02-29.abstract: Guillermo Padilla says he was part of a commando unit in 1973 that tortured and executed suspected opponents of General Augusto Pinochet.

2016-02-29.title: Twin Suicide Bombings Kill at Least 20 as Shabab Violence Intensifies in Somalia
2016-02-29.abstract: The Islamist militant group the Shabab said it carried out consecutive suicide bombings in the city of Baidoa that killed at least 20 people.

2016-02-29.title: In Toronto, a Neighborhood in Despair Transforms Into a Model of Inclusion
2016-02-29.abstract: An ambitious plan for the 69-acre Regent Park neighborhood is disrupting entrenched notions of class, race and religion, at a time when concerns over income inequality and immigration are growing.

2016-02-29.title: Day 2 of Syrian Partial Truce Is Marred by Violence
2016-02-29.abstract: A number of airstrikes and artillery attacks, reported by the warring sides, demonstrated the challenges of even a limited deal.

2016-02-29.title: Pakistan Hangs Guard for Assassination of Outspoken Secular Politician
2016-02-29.abstract: Malik Mumtaz Hussain Qadri fatally shot Salmaan Taseer, the governor of Punjab Province and a staunch opponent of the country’s blasphemy law.
